title: Pro account architecture
selection_reason: Backend billing, entitlement, webhook, and infrastructure planning triggers architecture guardrails; delegated to a bounded docs-only worker while implementation stayed local.
validations: Architecture includes Cloudflare-first path, Render fallback, data model, routes, secrets, webhook lifecycle, Klaviyo events, security/privacy constraints, rollout beads, validation, rollback.
notes: Committed and pushed by sub-agent before Bead 23A implementation commit.

title: Brand landing, documentation elements, blog stub, and pro checkout scaffold
selection_reason: Single Worker app surface with frontend, docs, and bounded checkout/Klaviyo scaffolding; no schema or entitlement grant changes were implemented.
fallback_agent: Frontend Engineer plus Backend Engineer split
tests: curl local /api/health, /blog, static asset, and POST /api/checkout
validations: MagicPath Brutalism/Ramp themes inspected and translated into court-image palette and copy feel.
Bibe Code landing and checkout/Klaviyo flow inspected as reference only; no secrets copied.
Desktop and mobile local rendering checked with no horizontal overflow.
POST checkout fails gracefully when Stripe is not configured.
notes: Stripe entitlement, webhook persistence, and Pro account storage intentionally deferred to the architecture bead sequence.

title: Deploy refreshed landing page to Cloudflare Workers
selection_reason: Deployment/runtime behavior changed and required direct Wrangler deploy plus live smoke checks against Cloudflare Workers.
validations: Cloudflare Worker baseline-ai deployed version 5cc879a3-983d-4e59-a620-e8abd8d70a99.
Live URL https://baseline-ai.ryan-borker.workers.dev served refreshed landing page and blog stub.
Live health returned db:true, stripe:false, token_required:true, lifecycle_email:false.
Live uploaded image asset returned HTTP/2 200.
Rollback path is Cloudflare Workers version rollback to the prior deployed version from the Cloudflare dashboard or Wrangler deployment history.
notes: Stripe and Klaviyo remain unconfigured on production, so checkout displays the expected unconfigured Stripe error instead of creating sessions.

title: Cloud Pro accounts, remote MCP, billing lifecycle, and macOS hotspot app
selection_reason: Schema, auth, public API, billing, deployment, remote MCP, and macOS app guardrails all applied; user asked to implement the full plan and then explicitly asked to evaluate it against the revenue/MCP/docs/Stripe skills.
fallback_agent: Architect + Backend Engineer + DevOps Engineer + macOS Engineer split
tests: local Worker smoke for /api/health, /docs/mcp, /mcp unauth challenge, and checkout fails closed
live POST /api/checkout fails closed without Stripe secrets
validations: Cloudflare Worker + Neon remains canonical; Supabase and local-only MCP were not used.
Stripe Checkout is preferred over payment links when account metadata-capable Checkout is configured.
Stripe webhook verifies raw signature, dedupes event ids, handles checkout/session/subscription/payment-failed states, and writes audit/lifecycle rows.
Workspace tokens are stored as prefix plus HMAC hash only; account-scoped ingest resolves account/workspace server-side and requires runs:write scope.
Remote MCP exposes seven bounded tools with auth challenge, discovery hints, next_actions, and confirmation for token revocation.
SwiftUI macOS app builds and uses remote MCP first, Keychain storage, local provider bridge, and OpenRouter fallback.
Skill audit recorded against revenue-plumber, stripe-checkout, apollo-ecommerce-router, churn-prevention, mcp-server-design, and documentation-website-for-software-project.
Deployed Worker version dfc2198f-9151-4a64-8511-4e25d3c2d529 passed live smoke.
notes: Production Pro remains fail-closed until MAGIC_LINK_SECRET, TOKEN_HMAC_SECRET, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PRICE_ID_PRO, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and Klaviyo secrets are configured. Lifecycle outbox rows are written but a retry/drain worker is not implemented yet.

title: Landing A redesign and BrandOS runtime repair
agent_selected: Primary Codex frontend and deploy operator
selection_reason: The bead changed the public Cloudflare Worker homepage and deployed production while preserving live cloud account and remote MCP routes; the primary agent used frontend and DevOps guardrails in the isolated worktree.
validations: Cloudflare Worker baseline-ai deployed version 4f1b94a0-543a-4cb2-8207-62825fb29594.
Live URL https://baseline-ai.ryan-borker.workers.dev served the Landing A redesign with the supplied court robot assets.
Live health returned db:true, stripe:false, token_required:true, pro_auth:false, pro_tokens:false, stripe_webhook:false.
Live remote MCP docs remained available after the homepage redesign.
Live hero asset returned HTTP/2 200 and checkout failed closed while Stripe secrets remain unset.
Playwright desktop and mobile layout checks found no horizontal overflow and no broken images.
BrandOS scripts now run on this machine without bare python, PyYAML, or a native prose CLI.
notes: The Worker deploy intentionally preserved Bead 25 cloud account and remote MCP route support from the active worktree before applying the Landing A homepage. BrandOS repair is staged separately in the skills-library repo because that repo contains unrelated dirty work.

title: Main-ready integration of Bead 25 cloud/Mac functionality and Bead 27 Landing A
selection_reason: The merge combines sibling branches that touched deployment docs, account/MCP architecture, and macOS app packaging while preserving the already-deployed Worker surface.
validations: Conflict resolution preserved Bead 27 Worker source while restoring Bead 25 macOS app, docs, Makefile verify-all path, and skill audit artifact.
handoff/beads.jsonl contains one valid entry each for 23B, 23A, 24, 25, 27, and this integration record.
Local Worker smoke returned the expected Landing A copy, remote MCP docs, 401 MCP auth challenge, protected-resource metadata, static asset 200, and Stripe-unconfigured checkout response.
PR #1 opened against main: https://github.com/apollostreetcompany/baseline/pull/1.
notes: No new production deploy was made for the integration branch because Worker source did not differ from the already-deployed Bead 27 Worker surface. Merge PR #1 after repository checks are green.

title: Deploy Baseline to trackbaseline.com custom domain
selection_reason: Cloudflare custom-domain routing, DNS, canonical app origin, and production Worker deployment changed; DevOps guardrails applied through deploy-ops.
tests: dig NS/A/AAAA for trackbaseline.com and www.trackbaseline.com
validations: Cloudflare Worker baseline-ai deployed version 0d0924c3-5c8e-4029-9327-369a73588786.
trackbaseline.com and www.trackbaseline.com are attached as Worker custom domains.
DNS nameservers are bingo.ns.cloudflare.com and harlan.ns.cloudflare.com; apex and www return Cloudflare A/AAAA records.
Worker APP_URL is https://trackbaseline.com, and MCP docs plus OAuth metadata now use the apex domain.
workers_dev is explicitly true and preview_urls is explicitly false, keeping the previous workers.dev fallback route available without preview URL ambiguity.
Live checkout still fails closed while Stripe secrets remain unset.
notes: Share https://trackbaseline.com. Rollback code path is wrangler rollback to 4f1b94a0-543a-4cb2-8207-62825fb29594; rollback domain path is removing custom-domain route entries and redeploying.

title: Public distribution and production Pro activation
selection_reason: The bead touched release distribution, public install docs, production Stripe/Klaviyo/auth secrets, Worker deployment, and checkout smoke; deploy and revenue guardrails applied.
tests: live unsigned Stripe webhook fail-closed smoke
validations: Worker version e38523fc-d11a-41d9-b05e-6dcef5f4b5f0 serves updated install docs and /install.sh on trackbaseline.com.
Production health returns db:true, stripe:true, lifecycle_email:true, pro_auth:true, pro_tokens:true, stripe_webhook:true.
Stripe prices are configured for Pro $39/mo and Team $129/mo; Stripe webhook endpoint we_1TYkp0G0PuTic3wegF1qVMpx verifies raw signatures.
GitHub Release v0.1.0 published macOS/Linux tarballs, checksums.txt, and baseline-openclaw-plugin.tgz.
Public installer downloads the release tarball, verifies checksum, installs baseline, and can run baseline doctor from a temp HOME.
npm wrapper package dry-run includes bin/baseline.js and can auto-download v0.1.0 into a temp HOME cache.
notes: The CLI binary stays free; Pro billing gates cloud history, workspace tokens, remote MCP account operations, monitoring, and retention. npm publish was not performed because npm whoami returned ENEEDAUTH on this machine.
